commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a dispatch helper that lets authorized native and text slash command turns bypass plugin-owned conversation binding delivery, plus regression tests for authorized and unauthorized `/codex detach` routing.

Reproducibility: yes. From source inspection on current main, a plugin-owned binding can run `runInboundClaimForPluginOutcome` and return `handled` before the normal reply resolver can process the control command.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is a narrow code/test repair to avoid bypassing plugin-owned bindings for unrecognized slash text.

target.title: Let plugin binding commands escape bound routes
target.body:
Fixes #85157.

## Summary

- Let authorized native command turns and authorized text slash commands with a parsed command name bypass plugin-owned conversation binding dispatch.
- Keep normal bound text, including unauthorized slash text, routed through the plugin-owned binding path as before.
- Add regression coverage for `/codex detach` escaping a plugin-owned binding before the plugin inbound claim path can consume it.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: plugin-owned conversation bindings were targeted before the normal command resolver path. A bound plugin session could therefore claim `/codex detach` as ordinary inbound text before OpenClaw command handling saw it.
- Missing detection / guardrail: the existing dispatch coverage verified ordinary bound text routing but did not cover an authorized text slash command inside a plugin-owned binding.
- Contributing context: Codex app-server bindings publish `/codex detach` as the detach hint, so the detach command must escape the plugin-owned inbound claim path.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: In a Discord conversation with an active plugin-owned Codex app-server binding, authorized `/codex detach` now escapes plugin binding dispatch and reaches normal command processing; unauthorized slash text still stays on the plugin-binding path.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/andy/openclaw/openclaw-85157` on branch `fix/plugin-binding-command-escape-85157`, running the actual `dispatchReplyFromConfig` implementation with the real session binding service and a registered plugin-owned binding adapter.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-85188-real-proof.mts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```json
{
  "proof": "plugin-owned binding command escape",
  "checkout": "/Users/andy/openclaw/openclaw-85157",
  "command": "node --import tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-85188-real-proof.mts",
  "authorized": {
    "label": "authorized /codex detach",
    "authorized": true,
    "bindingTouched": 1,
    "resolverCalls": 1,
    "pluginFallbackNoticeCount": 0,
    "finalReplyText": "resolver reached for authorized /codex detach",
    "result": {
      "queuedFinal": true,
      "counts": {
        "tool": 0,
        "block": 0,
        "final": 1
      }
    }
  },
  "unauthorized": {
    "label": "unauthorized /codex detach",
    "authorized": false,
    "bindingTouched": 1,
    "resolverCalls": 1,
    "pluginFallbackNoticeCount": 1,
    "finalReplyText": "resolver reached for unauthorized /codex detach",
    "result": {
      "queuedFinal": true,
      "counts": {
        "tool": 1,
        "block": 0,
        "final": 1
      }
    }
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The authorized `/codex detach` case touched the plugin-owned binding but emitted zero plugin fallback notices and reached the normal resolver path. The unauthorized `/codex detach` case touched the same binding and emitted one plugin-binding fallback notice, showing it stayed on the plugin-binding path instead of using the command escape.
- What was not tested: I did not connect this branch to a live Discord or Telegram account; the proof runs the OpenClaw dispatch path locally with a registered conversation binding adapter.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this patch, the plugin-owned binding block ran before command processing, so an authorized `/codex detach` message would enter the plugin binding claim/fallback path instead of escaping directly to command processing.

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
  - 1 file passed, 135 tests passed.
  - New regression coverage verifies authorized `/codex detach` skips `runInboundClaimForPluginOutcome` and reaches the reply resolver.
  - New guard coverage verifies unauthorized `/codex detach` still uses the plugin-owned binding path.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/commands.test.ts`
  - 1 file passed, 104 tests passed.
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
  - Passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
  - All matched files use the correct format.

I also verified commit attribution before pushing:

`git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`

Result:

`b58d53548e Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com> Let binding commands escape plugin routes`

If maintainers squash or rework this PR, please preserve author attribution or include:

`Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>`
target.author: turbotheturtle
